summary: Exact-HEAD release-risk review passed. The one-command closure converges through managed implementation, strict verification, independent review, bounded needs_changes repair, and final current-HEAD preparation while leaving PR creation, merge, waiver, and material external effects under explicit human authority.
inspection_summary: Inspected the gate request, Story/Architecture/Spec, production run-session and runtime ownership boundaries, public CLI surface, behavioral unit/integration/E2E coverage, strict-head verification ledgers, acceptance adjudication, architecture review, conformance delta, validation-sequence state, and current pr-prepare truth. Regression guard and path-surface coverage are satisfied. The code verdict is pass, but release readiness is still blocked: the canonical sequence has not recorded its current-HEAD final_review binding, and pr-prepare reports missing responsibility-authority evidence for unit_regression. These lifecycle blockers must be resolved and pr-prepare rerun; this review does not represent the branch as PR-ready.
inspection_inputs: node --test --test-name-pattern='same HEAD|needs_changes repair|final_prepare|legacy|provider fallback|cancel|resume' test/one-command-pr-ready-closure.test.js test/guarded-run-session.test.js test/independent-review-orchestrator.test.js
inspected_inputs: node --test --test-name-pattern='same HEAD|needs_changes repair|final_prepare|legacy|provider fallback|cancel|resume' test/one-command-pr-ready-closure.test.js test/guarded-run-session.test.js test/independent-review-orchestrator.test.js
judgment_delta: Regression guard: initial concern that the closure could optimize only the new happy path was resolved by exact-HEAD 288/288 unit proof, 4/4 integration proof, 4/4 E2E proof, and a focused 50/50 rerun spanning legacy profiles, migration, provider fallback, cancellation, resume, same-HEAD rejection, repair, and final preparation.

Path-surface coverage: initial concern that tests might bypass the public execution surface was resolved by coverage of CLI run/status/resume/cancel, persisted run state, managed worktrees, runtime providers, independent review lifecycle, repair replay, and current-HEAD final Gate preparation.

Workflow convergence: initial concern about stale evidence after implementation or repair was resolved by required HEAD advancement, authoritative Git HEAD checks, suffix replay from verify after needs_changes, bounded repair attempts, and final_prepare exact-HEAD rebinding.

External authority: initial concern that one-command autonomy could silently broaden authority was resolved by explicit rejection of injected authority seams and by the absence of PR creation, merge, waiver, deploy, publish, or other material external-effect actions from the autonomous DAG.

CI and readiness: initial concern that local completion could be mislabeled as PR-ready was resolved by fail-closed ci_pending/gate_recheck_required typed stops and by the current pr-prepare source of truth remaining ready_for_pr_create=false until outstanding exact-HEAD formal evidence is recorded.
release_readiness_caveats: The canonical validation sequence has not yet recorded the current-HEAD final_review phase/binding; the latest live sequence evaluation is needs_evidence.
The current pr-prepare reports gate:responsibility_authority blocked because unit_regression evidence is missing for a matched responsibility contract.
Gate lifecycle closure and a fresh pr-prepare are required before any PR-ready or CI-ready claim.
